page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by the custom 64-core AWS Graviton processor. Utilizing the ARMv8-based Neoverse-V1 architecture, the CPU operates at 2.6 GHz with 64 dedicated physical cores and a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It features a high-performance network interface delivering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance, meaning storage requirements must be met via network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core capabilities and memory subsystem performance. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core Geekbench tests and software compilation tasks using Clang. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block reads reaching 6,189.87 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 277.09 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, as are Passmark database operations and Redis SET throughput, which registers at 101.71 million operations per second. However, single-core metrics, such as single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core scores, remain in the average tier. Large-model LLM text generation also exhibits moderate performance, with text generation for a medium 7B model reaching 27.37 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7gn.metal offers high resource density and memory bandwidth relative to its ARM-based hardware class, making it a viable alternative to traditional x86 architectures for parallelized workloads. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, focusing the hardware's value entirely on raw compute and high-speed networking. This profile is highly suited for network-intensive and compute-heavy workloads, including high-performance computing (HPC), software compilation, static web serving, and distributed database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by a 64-core AWS Graviton processor built on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 architecture. Operating at 2.6 GHz with dedicated physical cores, it features 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network interface. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Benchmark results demonstrate top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, particularly in software compilation and database operations, though single-core performance remains average. This configuration offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized, network-heavy workloads. The high-speed networking and memory throughput make it optimal for processing large-scale network traffic and running distributed key-value stores like Redis, where it achieves 101.71 million operations per second.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring a 64-core ARM Graviton processor, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.metal is a 64-core ARM-based bare-metal server featuring 128 GB DDR5 RAM and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A dedicated ARM-based bare-metal server delivering high-speed networking, DDR5 memory bandwidth, and optimized multi-core compute performance for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high throughput
Top-tier multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for network-intensive workloads, web serving, and distributed databases
